channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: /SMG/ — 249 replies — 996.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/SMG/

Open thread • Replies: 249 • Updated: 2026-07-05 18:41 UTC

OP

1 more day wagies.

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html

https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

>>62445881

you have been tipped

62447680
BSX

62448282
>>62448277

62448284
>>62448277

The golden age is here.

62448285
>>62448281
I buy shares smigrrr
I don't buy options because I have shit luck in and out of the casino

62448297
>>62448272
What even is this for? his arch? a triumph over nothing. I guess will do a victory triumph for pretending to defeat iran.

62448298
>>62445593
I was gonna say I want this but then I saw the bogged looking tits....no thanks man

62448299
hi kevin please

62448307
>>62446923
I think I'll be staying out MU for the foreseeable future. Anytime those reddit losers jump in, it almost always spells the end of a pump....and writes the start of the dump.
>DRAM listed
Oh yeah reddit lurks here, I was wise to not share my alpha on here.

62448313
>>62448248
;_;
The happy quality family to single at 30 pipeline....

62448314
>>62448272
god you subhuman commies are such a blight, why are you parasites shilling on this board?

Are you gonna complain that the money that gets put into maintaining the Leaning tower of Pisa is a "waste of tax dollars" too retard?

62448315
>>62448272
contractor in chief

62448319
>>62448205
Maybe, but Friday was indeed green. 1.5% for me, which is pretty big considering everything.

62448321
TRLV

62448335
>>62448321
I just don't think they have much room to grow without removal from schedule 1. That could be 10 years from now.

62448337
Nothing ever happens...vuntil something happens. *nods wisely*

62448342
>>62448264
I knew a woman whomst
>early 20s
>pretty but extremely low self esteem
>virgin
>never had a bf
>would get random people (men and women) talking to her in public, she didn't realize the significance of this
>meets a guy
>*less than 1 year later*
>married
Yeah women don't have it hard even when they believe otherwise. I go out shopping and I do not have people just striking up conversation with me like that every single time, excluding cashiers.

At least getting rich will be one thing off the list that gets me more than having a wife would. Freedom from the wagecage. I'm so desperate for freedom, I hate waging. I just want my holdings to 20x so I can be free of the wagecage. So I can just sit down and not think "9 hours until hell rebegins." I want to just go "Tomorrow the market is open, maybe I will do some trading or I can just play video games, I'm already wealthy anyways." I want that freedom more than I want a gf or wife. The irony that this is now a stronger likelihood of happening over me getting a quality wife. Imagine going from $50k to a networth of $5m is seen as easier than going from single to married....

62448343
This cost 1 share of soxl

62448345
>>62448343
What will 1 share of soxl buy in a week?

62448349
>>62448343
You got ripped off, I haven't seen a fraud like that since Madoff.

>>62448345
It'll buy a night with your mother and your sister with some change to spare.

62448351
>>62448342
once you reach a certain income level, getting a gf/fiancee/wife is not hard at all- on the condition you are willing to relinquish all of your productivity also give total or near-total financial control to her

if you make enough money through your income and/or trading to be able to afford a mortgage on a house in a suburb with granite countertops and 1-2 international vacation trips a year- and if you're willing to text her heart emojis every 20 minutes, pretty much every woman is willing to date you as long as you're not literally Quasimodo. and that's not exaggeration or hyperbole or chudcel rhetoric. it's just the current meta.

62448352
>>62448345
Hopefully 3 of these because i have a lot more scheduled

62448353
>>62448349
>It'll buy a night with your mother and your sister with some change to spare.
So it'll capitulate. Selling all my semis on the open.

62448354
Things are so crazy. You may see this crazy mother fucker as president eventually Kek.

62448357
>>62448343
>eating alone

62448370
>>62448354
I'm not voting for Commander in Crack and I doubt anyone would, especially since the laptop fiasco was actively buried by the FBI at Bidens request.
This shit is admission of guilt by projection.

62448374
>>62448357

Always a hater.

62448383
>>62448357
>nooooooo dont eat heckin nice food if you dont have a date you get mcgoyslop instead!!!!!

62448391
>>62448357

If you think about it, why not? If you're out of town, what are you going to eat? Either in a restaurant or hotel or fastfood or you buy groceries LMAO

62448396

item.title: Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed. — 23 replies — 76.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed.

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-07-05 18:24 UTC

OP

Today is freedom day in burgerland, this means the CLARITY ACT has officially passed.

Now many crypto's like XRP and Chainlink are no longer illegal and the banks can start buying it.

Are you ready for the pump of a lifetime in the coming weeks?

Early replies (first 3)

62444894
I just took 70mg of illicit amphetamine sulfate

62444895
>>62444894
That's very nice to hear, I hope you are enjoying yourself.

62444904
>>62444895
it's nothing like meth (oral ROA meth had no euphoria either), minimal vasoconstriction; none really. not euphoric like adderall either, it feels like super non-diuretic caffeine. this has a 1:1 dextro:racemic ratio; so the pharma equivalent is evekeo.

I thought it was bunk but my heart rate is elevated but no arrhythmia like meth (that shit is poison, it'll keep you up for 3-5 days with no perceptual fatigue at significant cost)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62444906
>>62444895
I'm just irritated unless im working or reading something. which is the intended effect, it's just super mechanical caffeine idk how to describe it. it's impossible to "idle" on this shit, I have to be doing something.

62444911
>>62444904
>>62444906
okay well thats nice and all but not really what my thread was about

62444922
>>62444911
hm, do you know how it impacts privacy coins like XMR or even zioncash (ZEC)?

62444929
>>62444922
i do not

62444934
>>62444929
alright, I appreciate it regardless. thoughts on CBDCs?

62444939
>>62444934

I actually dont know much about that also

62444940
>>62444939
you sound like a nice guy no homo

62444948
these 2 got married y'know

62444959
>>62444940
Haha thanks man you too

>>62444948
Nooo we are just friends haha

62444978
>>62444892
> CLARITY ACT
wasn't there some announcement announced for yesterday?

62444991
op they haven't voted on it and aren't close to voting on it. they're hoping to get it done before august 10

62444995
>>62444991
Somone told me clarity act was 4th of july a few weeks ago

62445015
>>62444948
naw, the no homo made marriage impossible
>>62444959
>we are just friends
this

62445147
>>62444978
No, Trump did an interview restating that the US will be the crypto capital of the world.
>>62444892
CLARITY will pass, but there is a lot going behind the scenes that's causing it to stall. Did you know that CLARITY allows banks to buy and sell crypto? The last time US banks traded stocks was before the great depression.

62445160
>>62445147
Oh that's very interesting, thanks for sharing my friend

62445194
So, two more weeks?

62445212
>>62444948
you're responding to a samefag trying to keep his thread at the top because no one cares.

>>62444892
no one cares

62445217
>>62445212
that's not me

62445568
Why isnt crypto pumping harder?

62448360
>>62444892
The banks can already buy both. ETF inflows broke records despite being from small issuers on XRP, highest of any asset on the year, while inflows are borderline non-existent for link.

item.title: Line will not go down ever again — 9 replies — 36.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Line will not go down ever again

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-07-05 18:40 UTC

OP

I think i've just realized something looming over all western markets: we are past the era of market corrections. The line will never go down again.

Check out picrel's graph of the German stock index since 1988. After the dotcom hype of the 90s, the German DAX index topped out at around 7.500. A number it would take seven years to reach again. Thtas how long economic bust and boom cycles would naturally take in the markets.

Next time it would reach 7.500, it would be in 2007, shortly before the global (technically western) financial crisis. It would contract by about 50% but instead of taking seven years, it would only take four years to overtake the former record high.

But now look at what happened ever since: the DAX went from this multi decade high of 7.500 to the current 25.700 within just 13 years. In no way has economic output increased threefold in that time, so something else must be the reason.

After the fallout of the financial crisis, quantitative easing became a standard measure in western economies, to "stabilize" markets. This came at the cost of long term inflationary tendencies. Even after markets were past their old records, the governmental bodies decided to keep going. This had the effect of an ever larger amount of inflationary liquidity driving the stock markets higher indefinitely.

So this is where we are now. The one thing that made me suspicious about the situation, is how the German chancellor recommended to his people to put money into markets. I suspect this comes out of a need to further continue the line going up. Governments face massive inflation eversince the covid pump. It should have corrected markets by 60%, but that was not allowed to happen. As they did in 2008, inflation was chosen as a measure to keep valuations high.

Even more insidious, in a time, where young people are investing like never before, it seems that getting into wealth building in markets is harder than ever before.

Early replies (first 3)

62448196
-cont-

Current situations with the broader power dynamics in the world shifting towards BRICS has me quite sceptical of the current valuations of the western markets. The fact that the people in charge are desperate for te public to put their savings in to markets. The problem that our governments are no longer able to mask the inflationary spending, used to prop up markets. The fact that large amounts of increase in net worth and living standards has gone to a tiny fraction of society, which is now begging for the common man to support their system.

The west has no way out.

Either continue printing money to maintain on paper-growth at the expense of destroying your own currency. Or face the reality that large parts of the shareholder's money is based on fake growth in the stock markets.

What do you think they will do? Begging people to invest their savings seems like a last straw to me. A desperate attempt to somehow keep markets alive.

62448198
>>62448187
No shit nigga. Babies first realization. Guess we all start something

62448212
>>62448187
who is DAX and why are they tracking his performance?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62448213
>>62448187
>how the German chancellor recommended to his people to put money into markets

Trust the government. Sounds like a really brilliant idea I wouldve never think of.

62448242
all the good germans died in ww2. modern g*rms arent real people until you guys decrimalize holocaust denying

62448257
>>62448187
>+100% from 2023 to may 2025
ok
>infinite crab since may 2025
boooooooring
kospi's daily +10% or -10% swings are way more interesting

62448286
>>62448196
Inflation kills your savings. Politicians (democracy™) obviously don't want to enrage large portion of population so by encouraging you to move your money to stock market they still can serve the donor class while keeping the middle class conform. Think of stock market as giant savings account.

Inflation is an act of theft committed by the government (central 'private' bank) against its own people so they have to ease the pain a bit to keep the people calm. It's just the reality of fiat system and this is the safest path that politician could choose.

62448317
>>62448286
>savings
Shut the fuck up grandpa. Putting your money under your mattress is some great depression tier bullshit. "Savers" are the dumbest cattle I've ever heard, you deserve to GET FUCKED. Money SHOULD GO TO PRODUCTIVE ASSETS like stocks, that's the whole fucking point. Fractional reserve banking is based because it at least allows the banks to get SOME SORT OF PRODUCTIVITY from retards that keep 5 figures in their checking accounts.
Inflation is based, no one should be rewarded for having their money sit their doing nothing.

62448402
>>62448187
Yeah ah libe best thing ever saaar, aped life safings into line basterrd!

item.title: Index funds are dead — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Index funds are dead

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-07-05 18:39 UTC

OP

Something like 70% of the American public has their money in the stock market.

Index fund investors (“Bogleheads bro!”) want you to believe that they’re all going to be rich because they put $500 a month into the market. This would essentially mean every single American would become rich just by DCAing into the US stock market.

Index investing is dead. The ONLY reason it worked in the past is because like 5% of Americans owned stocks. The ones that got in first benefited from everyone else piling in, exactly like Bitcoin.

Look at Bitcoin, all the Bitcoin retards made the same mistake of telling people it’ll just go up and up forever, now we’ve plateaued for the last 5 years and the Bitcoin trade is dead.

The only winners going forward will be good active investors. You’ve been warned.

Early replies (first 3)

62448308
It's all going to crash in the next 5 years, and economic pain will be unlike anything we've ever felt. So enjoy your gay stocks you fucking wealthsimple zoomer nigger.

62448320
>>62448306
It's so over for America saar! India superpower by 2030!

62448334
>>62448306
You're forgetting that since the 1970s and the birth of the Internet we've seen basically all major corporations become multi-national entities, access to the American markets overseas increasing, sovereign wealth funds worth trillions of dollars coming up with holding timelines that basically go to infinity since they're not constrained to the lifespan of a single person, etc.
The days of the USA markets being constrained to just America is fucking over. That's why the Buffett Indicator is such a worthless metric now. The USA has basically conquered the globe in terms of stocks, sure you'll get some thirdies teeth gnashing over this and crying about BRICS but fundamentally the Boglechads are still right and are still early.
>B-BUT THAT MEANS AMERICANS WITH STOCKS ARE GOING TO BE WEALTHY
Yeah, no shit. We're the best country on earth, of course we're wealthy.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62448339
Id still take index funds long term over any other investment vehicle even if the returns will be closer to 7% a year longterm. Thats about equal to the true rate of inflation

62448366
>>62448334
U niggers are printing our meme reserve currency.

62448372
>>62448334
>access to the American markets overseas increasing
This will gradually reverse as the disparity in access to capital between US and non-US firms widens.
It won't be a "huurr duur everyone dump on America" event, but policies will be tweaked and adjusted to make up for this difference. It already happens in some countries for pensions with providers being forced to invest in domestic companies. They will eventually apply the same "tax incentives" to retail investors too and push them towards domestic assets.
The same way countries started waking up to the stupidity of shipping all manufacturing overseas they will start to see allowing unrestricted flow of capital towards US equities was a mistake.

62448375
>>62448306
Index funds are always the end game, and you’re brain dead for thinking otherwise. You’re not bigger than the program.

62448397
>>62448372
>This will gradually reverse
Countries that do that are unpopular because people want their pension fund to grow and not be stuck floundering. Look at Spain, the ruling socialist party has tried to buddy up hard to China and now the USA aligned party has seen their largest gap in polling in their favor. If anything, the trend is only going to swing in the USA markets favor because people want access to the most productive and predictable market.
>>62448366
And yet still gaining value compared to the Euro. Curious.

item.title: and just like that, CHAINLINK just revolutionzed WEALTH and FINANCE for BILLIONS of Africans BOOM — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
and just like that, CHAINLINK just revolutionzed WEALTH and FINANCE for BILLIONS of Africans BOOM

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-07-05 18:41 UTC

OP

and just like that, CHAINLINK just revolutionzed WEALTH and FINANCE for BILLIONS of Africans
BOOM

Early replies (first 3)

62446878
is that lil yachty?

62447583
>>62446852
This is the second video I've seen of his where he really doesn't understand what Chainlink's role is in whatever announcement. Here's he's blowing up CCIP to be bigger than it really is. The more important integration would be CRE which onboards the banks individual tools to pull data from for their smart contracts. If CCIP is involved, that's likely for token transfers or each bank needing to hook up their own blockchain to this system.

62447593
>>62446852
Why you keep posting this speed up videos that nobody can read?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62447898
>>62447593
All you need to see is the African.

62448381
>>62446852
I take all my investing advice from niggers frfr

62448398
>>62446878
Nah that's Lil' Bagholdy.

62448406
>>62446852
They linked up on god??

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-07-05 17:57 UTC

OP

Which one of you sold 2.1% of her token today for $1,300 bux?

How can you sell this old White girl, you should be ashamed. Take a good hard look in the mirror right NOW!

(I am not home atm, so at least I won’t be shoah’d from 4chan again for this thread for another week on my home internet)

62444972
>>62444945
i love she

62445037

She wouldn't lick the peanutbutter of my balls so she had to go, I'm sorry.

62445045

62446550

62446560
I will be honest. received an airdrop. Made me 20k. Ever since, I bought again. Do you think I care about such pocket change? Or do you think I understand how to winmax you retards? And next time that guy s around forewarn me.

62446579

Holy esl your skin is brown

62447209

OP

XRP is dead! 18 months ago, XRPScan showed approx. 10-15 XRP burned per minute. Now it takes 10-15 minutes for a whole XRP to be burned. Usage has plummeted.

t. seething linkie

62448401

item.title: A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn… — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn…

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-07-05 18:30 UTC

OP

A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn $1 per 1 LINK per DAY through staking. Does that mean if I’ve staked 2000 LINK, I’d earn $2000 per day?
Is that true, or was the anon just talking bullshit?

Early replies (first 3)

62448322
>>62448316
Just bullshit. At 4% annually your link would need to be worth $9125 per token.

62448378
>>62448316
He is just talking bullshit. They just pushed all their users to pay through stripe. There is not really a pathway to imagine corporate successes translating into token holder value. Payment abstraction layer and staking were both a flop and they were what link holders always said would be the way they would make it but now instead they deleted the past marketing materials, disallowed comment on it in their AMA and pushed Stripe payments. It has unironically never been more obvious and is so obvious it is borderline in the exit phase

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: House buying — 50 replies — 60.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
House buying

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-07-05 17:52 UTC

OP

I have $180k sitting in an S&P500 index fund and every normie I meet says I'm making a mistake by not getting a mortgage ASAP because housing will only get more expensive.

What is the /biz/ philosophy on this advice? I'm a rentoid and currently pay only around $1000 a month.

Early replies (first 3)

62447251
>>62447246
>every normie I meet says
You could buy and then the market severely crashes.
Housing shouldn't be a financial asset but that's corporatism for you.

62447257
>>62447251
Yea i guess but the stock market "could" also crash and i lose everything. That's not really insightful advice.

62447265
Lmao you are a rentoid

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62447346
>>62447246
Keep doing what you are doing. Don't listen to the normies. Their house is the only investment they've ever had and it made them money, so they think it's the greatest thing ever. They have no idea that stocks outperform housing every time, because they've never held any stocks.

62447355
>>62447346
part of the problem with your calculations is you're pretending the entire cost of the house can be invested

of course this isn't true because you're still paying rent and that's a 100% loss. So the only portion of your payment that would go to stocks is whatever the difference is between your rent and the mortgage payment. Which usually isn't much.

like if your mortgage is the same as your rent you gain nothing by renting. If your mortgage is $400 more than rent all you gained is whatever the difference is on that $400.

so it depends, but generally long-term buying will always beat renting because rent is a straight loss while the mortgage makes you something.

62447361
>>62447346
also of course by the time you're 20 years into your mortgage, it's going to be MUCH LESS than rent. I currently pay $800/month for a house that would easily rent for $4k per month.

over time the rent goes up while the mortgage remains static. It actually becomes cheaper because inflation eats away at the cost.

62447365
>>62447355
You forgot the down payment. If you put 50k down on a house vs. 50k in the stock market. In 25 years 50k turns into $541,000 in the stock market. That's most of the value of the house right there.

62447374
>>62447365
true, you're right about that. I forget it because I did an FHA with 3% down. At the time I paid $3k down.

google says that would be worth at best around $30k right now vs. a house that's worth over half a million.

also again, as rent goes up and my house payment remains the same I can put increasingly large amounts of cash into other investments.

62447376
>>62447365
also I'm not advising anyone to buy a house. It really depends on the location and how long you stay in it whether it's worth it or not.

over long time spans it has certainly paid off for me, but I don't know what will happen in the future. I do know prices are very high compared to wages right now. But I don't know if that will improve over time or not.

62447379
>>62447376
for example when I bought in 2002 my house cost 3 years wages in my area. Currently it would cost 12 years wages in my area.

based on that alone it's far less affordable to buy a house than it used to be.

62447387
I'm single so I feel like buying a house would be a waste. I also don't plan to live in my area for 15 years.

I do have a VA loan available since I'm a veteran but I don't think that makes a massive difference since I would pay off most of the principle in cash.

These 250k houses in my area look like absolute shit, plus the property tax rate is very high in my area.

I would end up evaporating more cash to live in a completely paid off $250k house (in some shitty suburb) every month then renting my apartment in the middle of the city.

62447395
>>62447387
that's another great point. If you don't need a house then it's probably not worth buying one especially if you plan on moving before the break even point. You'd be spending more money on space you don't need.

overall I tell my kids not to buy if they're not going to stay in place at least 10 years.

I also have VA available but FHA has always been a better deal for me. VA is great for buying a house you plan on renting out, but I've only done that once. If you're not going to rent the place out an FHA may actually be cheaper. In my experience the FHA loans are also much easier than the VA.

62447406
>every normie I meet says I'm making a mistake by not getting a mortgage ASAP because housing will only get more expensive.

They always say this at the top

62447412
>>62447333
So as I said, real estate only goes up in America.
Forever.
Act accordingly.

62447420
>>62447395
Buying to rent seems like not a very good financial decision. I would pocket a couple hundred bucks a month when i could just keep the entire principle in stocks.

62447628
>>62447412
>real estate only goes up in America.
It doesn't though. It can go down and stay down for years
>but not forever
No, not forever. But you aren't going to live in that house forever, so the "muh forever" argument is utterly moot.

62447657
Keep renting until you can purchase outright

Closing costs are 10k+ these days and interest rates are 6%+. Also during negoiations, you may be able to shave an extra 10 to 20k off just because closing on a cash home takes under 2 weeks compared to mortgage which can take 6wk or more. Much easier on sellers so they accept less.

Closing costs on a home with no mortgage is generally the cost of an inspection ($500) plus $250 to the title company. Every other thing the bank normally requires can be skipped

62447660
The opinions of normalfags and people who say "rentoid" are worthless, the US is collapsing. You want 180k to become 1.8 million and then to leave this dump of a country.

62447702
>>62447628
>dips
>oh no no no
Better not invest and rent forever.

62447752
>>62447660
>the US is collapsing

That's an incel fantasy you find pmg losers saying to justify losing all their money to a scam

62447776
>>62447752
Correct. America has another 100 years minimum so long as the CIA and Palantir play things right (they will).

62447823
>>62447246
fast forward 20 years:

birth rates collapsed
anti-immigration sentiment at ath
boomers dying en mass
the west continues to go downhill

historically house prices have been tied to income. and now apparently AI is going to take all our jobs, so I guess you should take that into account as well. the only thing keeping house prices high is immigration. locals cannot afford 1 million dollars for a house. most western countries would have a negative growth rate without immigration. house prices don't go up endlessly for no reason. even if I could afford a house, I would not buy one. you are tied down, you have to maintain the house, protect the house, insure it, pay the property taxes, utilities.. pay pay pay pay fucking endless bleeding money

62447843
>>62447823
Waaaaaaaaaaaah im a victim Waaaaaaaaaaaah

62447847
>>62447776
And about 70 of those years will be like brazil/south africa. USA has always been like 80-90% White, without Whites paying and building everything the country ceases to exist.

62447851
>>62447246
housing is going to crash as fuck except for like maybe 10 zip codes, good luck

62447875
>>62447843
what

62447928
They just banned Blackrcok from snatching up properties. The plan seems to be to try to keep it stable until the boomers die then let it drop.

62447930
Way too much silly discussion about purchasing a house as an investment over renting. You purchase a house to be the owner. IE I shoot guns and hunt in my back yard as I am the owner of the property. I could outright pay the entire mortgage but at 3% interest I choose to invest this money instead.

62447973
>>62447847
Look buddy, I'm a white supremacist literal Nazi who is so racist I left the USA and moved to a 100% white part of Eastern Europe - white people ain't all that special. We have a small .001% of brilliant world changing white men and the rest are mid as fuck.

62448058
>>62447246
Of all the stupid pics you could of chosen for your thread you choose that one? What the fuck for? Fucking faggot.

62448220
if youre in the 30+% fed brackets and youre not itemizing a big ass mortgage deduction why even live

62448304
>>62447361
Just know that at any point in time you could be renovicted or kicked out because the landlord (or their family) wants to move in. After they live there for a while, they will increase the rent to the market rate. Then your stuck finding a place at 3x rent.

62448311
>>62447843
>Just shut up and buy my bags, goy

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 128 replies — 55.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 128 • Updated: 2026-07-05 16:23 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62445628
>>62445569
Amy's blowjobs must suck

62445645
>>62445619
ABC had a space last week that had 500+ people watching. I'd imagine at least 10% of them were hedgies, analysts and silly shillies. It's a huge undertaking for retail to sit down and parse through these dockets to figure out what is happening. Wallstreet is well versed in this shit and have a dedicated team who analyzes the happenings of every stock out there. It's no surprise that Cifu started crying on Twitter because once he saw they were outflanked he knew immediately that the game was over and he blew his company up. They were giving money away for pennies because the barcode algo was taking the price to zero and was not phased in any way to price in what was about to happen. If the price was allowed to run back in February 6 2023, who knows where we could have ended up. Someone was getting squeezed back then and they decided to double, triple, quadruple down on the play because they were already bust. BBBYQ was already infiltrated and sabotaged and the short interests were already well outside any semblance of prudence by risk management standards. All that was left to do was to physically short the stock with impunity until it was near $0 and hope the debts were too much for anyone to try to buy them up.
But they were too little too late.

62445678
GUYS RYAN COHEN JUST REPLIED TO MIKEHANDS ON X. MIKEHANDS IS FAMOUS FOR BEING A REGULAR ON THEPPSHOW. RYAN LITERALLY CONFIRMED TODAY THAT WE WON. HOLY FUCKING SHIT

62445683
>>62445678

62445686
ABC is such a gargantuan retard, don't give him the credit of "parsing documents." He literally reads off an AI script verbatim and just rehashes the same theory everyone else is already aware of. He also tried to pivot into being this startup business X account and had to stop that nonsense fast when he realized how literally no one wants to listen to his mindless drivel. Now he's trying to be the mysterious "I know something you don't" type guy with a Carl Icahn profile picture, because those two are totally the same person.

God, I hate everyone in this play so fucking much. PP's also a massive retard, but at least that's his brand, and it's somewhat upfront.

62445700
>>62445686
Now that's what I call a Silly Shilly!

62445705
>>62445700
im not even a shill, as i also contemplate the complications of the CUSIP Conundrum. i even emails Cusip Global Services about it in March and they never got back to me--how very curious. that said, i am so sick of these retards on twitter pretending like they're doing anything but peddling false hype. they don't even bring up the CUSIP-6 thing which is fucking insane to me because that is a piece of evidence staring us right in the face for months now which has very interesting implications.

62445706
>>62445686
ABC? You mean Alfred?

62445735
>>62445619
relax.
He does software engineering (hes actually been stuck in tech support for 6 years).

He can hack you (him hacking you is just making shit up and alphastate reposting it)

62445743
>>62445686
Jake already confirmed we won, so it doesn't matter what anyone else says.

62445859
>>62445743
did anyone else itt literally cum when they saw that picture of him on the couch

62445900
>>62445859
Yea but like, most of us cum every day at least.

62445941
>>62445705
A silly shilly
Being a seethy weethy
Built for GME

62446010
>>62445859
I'm going to frame the picture of Jake on the couch and put it up in my mansion. When guests will ask who he is, I'll smirk and say "you wouldn't get it."

62446088
>>62445569

62446106
>>62445576
>>62445600
>>62445645

spot on

62446149
>>62446088
They leave trading open so smart people can sell "for tax purposes" and get the loss on their tax return. I guess they think idiots buying 7 biĺion worthless securities for 8 cents each is funny,,,,,i do

62446238
>>62445569
what is this image even from?

62446242
>>62446238
wait I found it:

https://www.timesofisrael.com/us-hockey-team-owner-suspended-after-social-media-spat-with-fan-over-gaza-war/

HOLY SHIT ITS FROM 2024. Imagine what him seething at RC has done to him since?

62446288
>>62446242
Hes 60 years old whats the joke here?

62446344
>>62446242
<suspended


Lmao so nothing happened to him except a meaningless press release BBBYbbqers cant stop losing

62446386
>>62446344
the real fun starts when the rico roundup is in full swing. But even better is when cash and new equity is distributed to previous BBBYQ holders.

Guess what happens when tzero gets involved.

62446441
>>62446386
>Guess what happens when tzero gets involved.

Your $0 stays $0

62447283
CUSIP-6

62447659
Ryan Cohen confirmed AGAIN that bathroom gods have won by posting on X that shows he has ties to the Icahn family. Shills will immediately come out of the woodwork now that I bring this up. It's over shills, we won. It's been 100% confirmed.

62447686
>>62447659
it's not brett icahn's fiance the birthmarks don't match

62447756
Baggie general

62447918
>>62447756

62448131
lamestop holders are seething because they missed out on the true moass. bathroom gods are the chosen ones. they trusted the plan above all. the cancellation of shares was the ultimate IQ test. weaker people couldn't handle that. now they are filled with regret.

62448146
>>62448131
The silo around BBBY was truly impressive. Superstonk made no mention of it. /GME/ was a full blown silo as well. It wasn't until I looked at the option chain on BBBY and saw the insane volatility that made me look in to it further. Greatest decision of my life.

item.title: whats it like living in london? Chelsea looks unreal god damn if i was a billionaire i would live in london forever. Eve… — 33 replies — 50.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
whats it like living in london? Chelsea looks unreal god damn if i was a billionaire i would live in london forever. Eve…

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-07-05 18:03 UTC

OP

whats it like living in london? Chelsea looks unreal god damn if i was a billionaire i would live in london forever. Even if i had a few mill i would probably move there from USA.

Early replies (first 3)

62440816
Don't think you would, mate

62440835
>>62440727
Brilliant, best city in the world

62440843
>>62440727
lel london.

Moved away from Fulham and London 5 years ago thank fucking christ. Hell on earth.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62440865
>>62440843
Written by a jeet in Mumbai

62440873
>>62440727
its literal shit i genuinely dont understand what you find appealing about it ?

62440877
Check the demographic figures for London then come back here and tell us you still want to live there.

62440886
>>62440727
Not nice

62440968
>>62440727
Slave mind. But its good that you retards stay in the worst places in the world and leave thai beaches empty.

62440971
>>62440865
Mumbai and London is there a difference?

62440974
>>62440968
Thailand lmao how fucking depressing my god

62441031
>>62440974
>says the guy wanting to live in one of the worst cities in the world
>yeah pic related is depressing
>but concrete and a mix of jeets, niggers, moslems and assholes is good

62441040
A thread full of jeets seething because they don't live in London

62441046
>>62440727
>>62440816
>>62440835
>>62440865
>>62440873
Go back home. Nobody wants you to be in Europe.

62441083
>>62440727
Pretty sure it's like living in Pyongyang but browner

62441105
The trick is not to live in London but in the nice suburbs of the borders/surrounding home counties and enter London only for work and selected leisure

62441183
>>62441031
>he won’t ever respond to this

62444114
>>62441183
Yeah, it seems he left

62444152
Is this a troll post? What's so "unreal" about Chelsea?
Even if hustle and bustle is what you're after I'm pretty sure large American cities have more to offer given the higher purchasing power. 20% of Londoners are on unemployment benefits and then there's a large chunk of Londoners on full time employment who are too poor to afford anything and are also on benefits.
Add in all the illegal immigrants and non whites living under the radar and you get a city of 8 million people but the entertainment and night out options are there to cater for a city of 3 million or less because they're the only ones able to afford anything. When factor those things in London is about as vibrant as some mid-tier American city like Denver or Phoenix.

It's a bit like jeets bragging about the massive size of Mumbai not taking into account the fact Minneapolis has more people who are able to eat out once a week.

62444383
>>62440971
Mumbai has white people

62444388
>>62444152
I think I heard something like welfare obligations exceed tax receipts in the UK also, whole country is basically fucked

62444392
The UK is as poor as Mississippi, literally.

https://www.theatlantic.com/magazine/2026/07/uk-productivity-economy-reform-party/687303/

62445003
>>62440727
It's okay if you don't have to pay a mortgage or rent. The problem is it's overcrowded by the wrong people. Immigration from India is out of control and they are wrecking the place and turning it into a dump. Add to that there are Somalians everywhere and they contribute absolutely nothing. Their kids are animals. Then you've got the usual afro-black population growing (contribute nothing) but crime.

That said I've noticed an increasingly growing population of American elders here. They find the UK more peaceful and united than America apparently.

If you have a good job and earn above £45-50k, you can do ok, but not live expensively.

62447421
>>62440727
ANOBODY IS BUYING YOUR CHELSEA REAL ESTATE HAHAHHAHAHAHAHAHHAHHHAHAHAHAH

62447431
>>62441046
>britcuckstan
>europe

62447436
>>62440727
You're an incel innit?

62447551
London? England isn't even a real country anymore, if youre a lazy brown scammer it's probably the best place on earth

62447566
I lived there before Brexit and slept with many women from Eastern Europe that just arrived.

It was a great time to be in London.

I hear it's a crime filled shithole now.

62448147
>>62447436
what does that have to do with anything i jut really like london its a charming city.

Yes i am incel

62448178
>>62448147
That explains why. Also, why does it matter where you live if you never leave your house?
Live in Thailand and grow up.

62448193
>>62448178
how does that explain it?? london is for incels? huh i thought it was rich chads and women city is the migrant crisis that bad

62448252
>>62440727
Why on earth, anon? What makes you think London is a good place to live? NYC, LA, Miami are shitholes but I could understand if you picked places like that. See >>62444152

62448288
>>62448193
London is too social and cool for an incel like yourself gov, you're better off somewhere like Bangkok that'll pull you out of your shell innit

62448326
>>62448193
ur more likely to see a white person in middle of Cairo than London

item.title: Post country and how much PTO you get >aus >5 weeks holiday 2 weeks sick — 56 replies — 46.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Post country and how much PTO you get >aus >5 weeks holiday 2 weeks sick

Open thread • Replies: 56 • Updated: 2026-07-05 17:29 UTC

OP

Post country and how much PTO you get
>aus
>5 weeks holiday 2 weeks sick

Early replies (first 3)

62443952
US
"Flexible" but I took seven weeks last year

62443958
>>62443952

If you’re white collar professional is the USA really a nightmare for employees like everyone says ? Or it’s over exaggerated?

62443972
>>62443958
Depends on the company I guess. I've always imagined that your average retard that complains about a lack of PTO works fast food, retail, or something equally menial

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62445041
>>62444988

Yea I’m planning to, will just keep 5 days spare just in case

62446526
>>62443950
>Canada
>I work contract so maybe only work 6-8 months of the year then 4 months off with employment insurance but I've had my EI case open for like 5 years now, I just claim it every chance I get.

62446702
15 days per year of combined PTO + sick time as a burger tech worker. It was 13 days per year until I hit one year at the company.
I think I need to get a new job.

62447281
>>62443950
>Aus
>0 days annual leave
>0 days sick leave
>Unpaid public holidays
>No legal protections if they sack me
The advantage is that I get paid more than my colleagues, and I'm well-known enough in my industry that I'll never be out of work.

62447298
>>62443950
Latvia.
32 days plus unlimited sick leave and also I cam take paid sick leave to take care of my kids if they get sick.

62447300
>>62443958
You might have a huge salary but only 10 to 15 days paid time off and 0 sick leave. That's normal in America. And people are encouraged strongly to take 0 vacation days (can be "cashed out").

62447301
>>62444080
>holidays
Nobody was talking about holidays and now you're coping.
If the rest of us add in Holidays then we get like 50 days PTO a year.

62447310
Notice how America is right in-between or on par with most European countries, yet the doomer grifters on YouTube are always pushing shit about how Americans don't get any days off.

Same with the retarded air-conditioning debate. Now that Europeans are actually visiting Florida and Texas, they're like "oh, sorry Americans, we didn't realize you live in a fucking jungle hellscape".

62447320
>ITT: extreme lying and coping Amerifats

62447342
>>62443950
>CZ
>none, because I'm a business owner
>used to be 4-6 weeks + some sick days + national holidays when I was an employee
>used to be unlimited paid holiday when I worked for a certain gamedev studio as a contractor

62447350
>>62443950
Thank god I'm no longer a wagie but I used to get 27 days PTO which was actually only 20-21 after you account for holidays when the pharmacy was closed anyway. I had to work for 8 years to get to that point. Years 1 to 7 mean you get 5 fewer days. On top of that, there were a bunch of gay rules for when and how time off could get approved. I used to have to swap shifts with a bunch of other pharmacists if I wanted 3 weeks off in a row for international travel.

62447370
>>62447300
>>62447301
I'm in USA

I get 25 - 30 days of paid vacation. If I included Holidays it would be another 10 days. And I'm private sector. Government sector or financial/banking employees get even more holidays than that.

Quit with the seething.

62447386
>>62444126
>USPS
Were federal but independent
>wounded warrior leave 13 days
>annual leave 20 days/ 26 if you've been here like 15 years or something
>sick leave 13 days
>federal holidays are off

62447414
>>62447370
>>62447320
Curious.
I worked for American companies for decades. Northrop Grumman. Booz Allen. GD etc.
Mostly I had 10 to 15 days total PTO and 0 sick leave. With a small company I got up to 22 days total PTO after 5 years tenure.

62447425
>>62447414
Yes, it's based on years of service. Which makes sense. Some loser shouldn't be able to get hired, work for 1 month, take 2 weeks for, get paid, then quit.

62447617
Another amerifat here. I work in biotech. 4 weeks vacation, plus 1 week sick time, but were discouraged from using that unless we’re “””actually sick.””” Fed holidays too, plus they give everyone the week between Christmas and new years off. After one more year there I’ll get 5 weeks vacation.

62447636
>>62447320
People on /biz/ are generally more successful then your average wagie thus better benefits with their job. Your average wagie that talks about stocks is piling into something that already had parabolic growth and is due for a drawdown. Their PoV is worthless

62447666
>>62443950
5 weeks. American but yuro company.

>>62443958
Yes it's one reason I haven't changed jobs. 2 weeks is normal even for good jobs.

62447893
>>62447310
Yeah reading this thread was genuinely surprising. I finally visited Europe (Italy) for the first time over the last 2 weeks and while it was very nice, I don't think life in europe is way better than life in America like I was led to believe. They each have advantages and disadvantages. But with that said, it is a lot easier to accrue personal wealth here than there.
>>62447301
Yeah, I didn't count holidays either even though they're technically PTO.
>>62447298
Doesn't everyone have unlimited sick leave on paper at least? We're only talking about PTO.

62447964
>>62447893
I mean I have 4 kids and when they get sick the pediatrician writes me a note that gives me a week paid off work.

62447974
>>62447964
I can do the same. For up to 4 weeks i don't even need the note.

62447987
>>62447964
>>62447974
Oh, but after 4 weeks i do need the note and it stops being paid.

62447990
>>62447281
How many dicks did you have to suck to get your stellar reputation?

62448091
>>62447974
>>62447987
If I wasn't American I'd probably believe you but alas.

62448115
>>62448091
It's true. My manager is very relaxed about sick leave because he himself uses it every year.

62448199
>>62448115
/thread

Never once in my 15 years of work have I had a manager deny, or get pissed about me using my vacation days. Like I said earlier, this is all doomer nonsense people watch on YouTube or read on left-wing news headlines.

And the Americans that don't get paid leave (like waiters), have open work schedule, unlike factory workers. Waiters can take off or trade shifts with people whenever they want, they just don't get paid for it.

62448201
>>62447617
/thread

62448206
>>62444410
>Also how does it work if you leave the company? Here we get paid out our leave (not sick tho) in cash when we quit.
It's different state by state. In most states, companies don't have to pay out your vacation bank if you leave, but every time I had that I found the company was willing to pay it out.
On the unlimited side that means there's no bank to pay out, but once you get to that level it doesn't make much of a difference unless you leave willingly (at which point you probably got a signing bonus at the new job anyway) or if you get laid off you get a severance package, or if you were fired for misconduct or not doing your job they weren't going to pay it out anyway.

62448231
>>62447281
>nigger slave bragging about job security in the 1800s

62448269
i get 22 days + 12 company holidays + half day fridays in the summer. i don't know how many sick days i get, but i wfh so it's not a big deal.

item.title: VOO and Chill — 186 replies — 32.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 186 • Updated: 2026-07-05 12:58 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62428337
>>62428238
In other words you don't know

62429683
>>62400493
Anon there is nothing wrong with doing VOO and chill if you have time and principal
Going into wildly volatile ETFs can be okay if it’s a satellite account
Just be careful of FOMO and not being risk averse
You sound like you have a good head on your shoulders though
Remember this is money, real money, your money

62430693
VOO anons where you at?

62430902
>>62400493
Contrarian nerds always lose.
Always.
In middle school dating.
Being high school incels.
In college.
At work.
In love.
At investing.
At all times.
Follow the crowd and try to fit in. Everyone doing anything else is a reject loser.

62431131
>>62430902
Who is being the contrarian here though

62431374
VOO is managed by 2 girl bosses, Michelle Louie & Aurélie Denis. So with your 0.03% management fee you're essentially paying for those do-nothing "day in the life of a portfolio manager" videos for daughters of elite sociopaths.

62431402
>>62431374
A small price to pay for a low expense ratio

62431920
>>62431131
Anyone trying to beat VOO over decades.

62431922
>>62431920
Feel like VUG has consistently beaten VOO though

62431971
>>62431922
Contrarian.
I own VUG and don't own VOO tho :)

62432023
>>62431920
fair enough
thoughts on a younger person doing QQQM?

62434054
Anyone have thoughts or realized returns and experiences on VOO + satellite
accounts instead of just VOO

62435372
Was gonna try to go into this DRAM dip but decided to just do more VOO
But idk a higher return ETF still sounds promising and like a no brainer whether it’s SOXX or QQQM but QQQ has so much crossover with VOO it’s just redundant and only good for growth seasons
I guess this is why VOO and Chill exists to just stop thinking

62436303
>>62431920
What about QQQ though

62436357
i boughted my daily VOO
i'll keep dealing the real world like a good goyim in the mean time.

62436478
>>62436357
huzzah brother

62436499
>>62400493
Where my VGT bros at?

62436507
>>62400636
>Yes and I'm sure that used car and maybe a holiday you can buy with it when you're 65 from cashing out all your investments will be great.....
You say this as if the average /biz/ shitcoin gambler is performing better than VOO which is definitely not the case.

62436510
>>62428031
Your IQ is so actually low. Holy shit.

62436538
>>62436510
Do you think DRAM has value, honest question, and for how long - if so, what dip should one wait for
>>62436499
VGT and QQQ are only doing better thanks to tech having such strong performances for years now, correct?
VOO seems safe since you don't know the future, but on the other hand, thinking the future of the market for the next decades wont always be tech focused seems so short sighted it's silly.....maybe I should start DCA into VGT?

62436562
>>62400493
>VOO
it's EVOO for me lads

62438775
Yep bros
Bought some more VOO and am waiting on my DRAM position to go green so I can sell and not have to deal with thematic ETFs no high expense ratios ever again
Simple as

62440169
>>62431374
>VOO is managed by 2 girl bosses
VOO is passively managed. These 2 girls are not like Kathy Wood picking stocks.

62442752
>>62431374
They could boil alive eat grannies alive for all I care as long as they get the money going, what kind of faggot ass train of thought is this. They could become billionaires and I would not give a shit as long as it keeps compounding. "The people making me rich are foids so it makes me icky" is unironically the foidest take I have ever read.
>>62430902
A year ago I started investing for the first time in my life (I'm in my mid 20s) and thought I was very smart and I would just outperform the market 1% profit at a time. After a whole year I only managed to get a net benefit of a 3% after wasting many hours and carrying heavy bags. I learnt my lesson. VOO and chill.

62442988
>>62442752
>They could become billionaires and I would not give a shit as long as it keeps compounding.

The portfolio managers of VOO have 0 control over whether VOO compounds or not. VOO is simply an ETF that tracks the S&P 500. Their only job is to make sure VOO follows the S&P 500. Once the fund is set up, there really is not much for them to do. I guess if they haven't automated removing and adding companies to the composition of VOO, then they do that.

62444521
Let’s all say thank you to Jack Bogle

62444531
>>62400515
and yet boomers hold 90% of the wealth and growing...

62445651
>>62421799
>5+ yr 60% drawdown event happens
>buy cheaper voo

62446571
>>62444521
thanks chad bogle

62447653
>>62445651
Kek it really is that simple

item.title: Behold the Golden Crab in the room — 8 replies — 17.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Behold the Golden Crab in the room

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-07-05 18:14 UTC

OP

If you bought BTC at the end of 2017 you had purchased the value equivalent of around 15 oz of gold.
If you sold that BTC right now you could purchase... 15 oz of gold.
That's 9 years of bag holding to break even.
For most of the past 5 years, if you purchased Bitcoin, you actually lost value in gold.
What have we learned?
>bitcoin sucks for growing wealth
>fiat is garbage
>gold is the only real store of value of these three

Early replies (first 3)

62447252
>buy btc in 2021
>lose half my gold
btc maxis not like this

62447264
>>62447252
Gold lost 50% against the stock market this year

62447318
>>62447264
A good year for stock scams, but it looks like stocks are trending down overall to around what some might consider neutral compared to gold. If you bought the DJI in late 2017 (compared to Bitcoin) you lost half your gold value though.
https://www.macrotrends.net/1378/dow-to-gold-ratio-100-year-historical-chart

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62447341
>>62447318
You make 78 times more with stocks than rocks, go sell your poison elsewhere

62448249
>>62447341
You're in denial. Look at the link I posted. If you can't ourchase more gold with your "gains" then you didn't make any gains.

62448338
>>62447341
Wrong.

62448341
>>62447341
In 1929, \(\$100\) would buy approximately 4.84 troy ounces of gold.

62448344
>>62447341
At a live spot price of $4,187.30 per troy ounce, 4.3 troy ounces of pure gold is worth $18,005.39.
